---
title: "Spring phenology tier2 project"
author: "Constantin Zohner"
date: "Last run: `r format(Sys.Date(), '%d %B %Y')`"
subtitle: "Run moving-window models for fixed preseasons (PEP725 data)"

format:
  html:
    toc: false
    theme: default
    highlight-style: haddock
    df-print: paged
    embed-resources: true

execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
  echo: true
  output: true

always_allow_html: true
editor: visual
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: inline
---

<br>

### **Driver names**

Tmax.PS60: Mean maximum daily temperature 60 days prior to series-level mean leaf-out

Tmax.spring60: Mean maximum daily temperature 60 days prior to species-level mean leaf-out

<br>

# 1. Setup

<details>

<summary>Packages</summary>

```{r}
require(tidyverse)
require(data.table)
require(broom)
require(pbmcapply)
```

</details>

<details>

<summary>Directories</summary>

```{r}
#-------------------------------------------------------
# Define the base directory
#-------------------------------------------------------

base.dir <- "/Users/goalie/Desktop/PhD/Publication_material/40_Spring_phenology_tier2/PEP725_Analysis"


#-------------------------------------------------------
# Build specific directories from the base
#-------------------------------------------------------

PEP_MW.dir    <- file.path(base.dir, "Analysis_input/PEP_drivers_final_spring_MW/MW15/Merged_file")
PEP_clim.dir <- file.path(base.dir, "Analysis_input/Spring_climate")
output.dir <- file.path(base.dir, "Analysis_output/ModelResults/Moving_window_analysis/Individual_files_fixed_preseason_models")
```

</details>

<details>

<summary>Data</summary>

```{r}
#------------------------------------------
# Moving window data
#------------------------------------------

MW.df <- fread(paste(PEP_MW.dir, "pep_MW15_merged.csv", sep="/")) %>% 
  dplyr::select(c(
    # Identifiers
    s_id, year, MWyear, species, timeseries,
    # Phenology
    phenology, leaf_out, 
    # Climate (Mean maximum daily temperature 60 days prior to series-level mean leaf-out)
    Tmax.PS60)) %>% 
  # delete time series with too little variation in leaf-out dates (leaf-out SD ≤ 1)
  group_by(phenology, species, timeseries, MWyear) %>%  
  filter(sd(leaf_out, na.rm=T)>1) %>% 
  ungroup() 


#------------------------------------------
# PEP data for climate
#------------------------------------------

PEP_clim.df <- fread(paste(PEP_clim.dir, "pep_drivers_spring_climate.csv", sep="/")) %>% 
  dplyr::select(s_id, year, species,
                Tmax.spring60)


#------------------------------------------
# Merge Moving window and PEP data
#------------------------------------------

MW.df = MW.df %>% left_join(., PEP_clim.df, by = join_by(s_id, year, species))


# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# Global shift for log-transforming temperature
# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# Goal: avoid undefined log(T) when Tmax <= 0, while keeping the transform
# monotonic and *time-invariant* (same shift everywhere).
#
# We add a single constant so that (Tmax + shift) is always > 0:
# shift = |min(Tmax)| + 0.01  -> ensures the minimum becomes +0.01.
eps <- 0.01

min_PS60     <- min(MW.df$Tmax.PS60, na.rm = TRUE)
min_spring60 <- min(MW.df$Tmax.spring60, na.rm = TRUE)

shift <- ifelse(
  min(c(min_PS60, min_spring60)) <= 0,
  abs(min(c(min_PS60, min_spring60))) + eps,
  0
)

# print the shift value to the console for reproducibility
shift

MW.df = MW.df %>% 
  
  # remove time series with no variation in preseason temperature
  group_by(phenology, species, timeseries, MWyear) %>%  
  filter(sd(Tmax.spring60, na.rm=T)>0, 
         sd(Tmax.PS60, na.rm=T)>0) %>% 
  
  # Create transformed predictors/response used in the different model variants
  mutate(
    # Log-transform leaf-out timing (addresses "inverse scaling" in response space)
    leaf_out_log  = log(leaf_out),
    
    # Log-transform temperature after applying a global shift (avoids clipping artefacts)
    Tmax.spring60_log = log(Tmax.spring60 + shift),
    Tmax.PS60_log     = log(Tmax.PS60 + shift),
    
    # Z-score logged temperature within each MW group (removes variance-driven scaling in X)
    # as.numeric() ensures a plain numeric vector (scale() otherwise returns a matrix)
    Tmax.spring60_z_log = as.numeric(scale(Tmax.spring60_log)),
    Tmax.PS60_z_log     = as.numeric(scale(Tmax.PS60_log))
   ) %>% 
   ungroup()

rm(PEP_clim.df)
```

</details>

<br>

# 2. Analysis

<details>

<summary>Show</summary>

```{r}
#-------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Create equations
#-------------------------------------------------------

equation.list <- list(
  # St using mean Feb-Mar or Mar-Apr temperatures
  St_PSfix_abs = formula("leaf_out     ~ Tmax.spring60"),       # days per °C
  St_PSfix_log = formula("leaf_out_log ~ Tmax.spring60_log"),   # log-log transformed
  St_PSfix_z   = formula("leaf_out_log ~ Tmax.spring60_z_log"), # logged y, scaled x
  
  # St using mean temperatures 60 days prior to series-level mean leaf-out
  St_PS60_abs = formula("leaf_out     ~ Tmax.PS60"),      # days per °C
  St_PS60_log = formula("leaf_out_log ~ Tmax.PS60_log"),  # log-log transformed
  St_PS60_z   = formula("leaf_out_log ~ Tmax.PS60_z_log") # logged y, scaled x
)

# Equation identifier 
equation.names = names(equation.list)


#-------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Loop through equations
#-------------------------------------------------------

parallelCalc <- function(equation.name){ 
  
  equation <- equation.list[[equation.name]]
  
  ModelResults.df <- MW.df %>%
    group_by(phenology, species, timeseries, MWyear) %>%  
    do({
      df <- .
      model.linear <- lm(equation, data = df)

      # extract y and x from the model frame (handles transforms in formula)
      mf <- model.frame(model.linear)
      y  <- mf[[1]]
      x  <- mf[[2]]

      # variance components
      SD_y <- sd(y, na.rm = TRUE)
      SD_x <- sd(x, na.rm = TRUE)
      var_ratio <- SD_y / SD_x
      
      # means
      Mean_y <- mean(y, na.rm = TRUE)
      Mean_x <- mean(x, na.rm = TRUE)

      # simple correlation (robust to NAs)
      ok <- is.finite(x) & is.finite(y)
      r_xy <- if (sum(ok) >= 3 && SD_x > 0 && SD_y > 0) {
        cor(x[ok], y[ok], use = "complete.obs")
      } else {
        NA_real_
      }

      tidy(model.linear) %>%
        dplyr::filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>% 
        dplyr::mutate(
          equation  = equation.name,
          sd_y      = SD_y,
          sd_x      = SD_x,
          var_ratio = var_ratio,
          mean_y    = Mean_y,
          mean_x    = Mean_x,
          cor_xy    = r_xy
        )
    }) %>%
    ungroup()
  
  write.table(
    ModelResults.df,
    file = paste0(output.dir, "/", equation.name, ".csv"),
    sep = ",", row.names = FALSE, col.names = TRUE
  )
}


#-------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Run parallel code
#-------------------------------------------------------

#initialize the loop
outputlist <- pbmclapply(equation.names, parallelCalc, mc.cores=6, mc.preschedule=F)

#check how many files there are
length(list.files(path=output.dir, pattern='.csv'))
```

</details>

<br>

# 3. Reproducibility

<details>

<summary>Show</summary>

```{r}
## datetime
Sys.time()

## session info
sessionInfo()
```

</details>
